# EMILY's List is a community of over five million members that helps elect pro-choice Democratic women to office.

body:

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523c89/4067b0a/49d10070e/7e5e18f9/3377727397/VEsA/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20180106_dnc_v3s1&source=em_20180106_dnc_v3s1"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523c89/4067b0a/49d10070e/7e5e1886/3377727397/VEsB/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180106_dnc_v3s1&source=em_20180106_dnc_v3s1"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523c89/4067b0a/49d10070e/7e5e1887/3377727397/VEsO/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180106_dnc_v3s1&source=em_20180106_dnc_v3s1") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523c89/4067b0a/49d10070e/7e5e1884/3377727397/VEsP/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=9+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180106_dnc_v3s1&source=em_20180106_dnc_v3s1"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: The White House
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d8442452e9fd66dd0cfd6f4e8d857911c67dfc4cfc74c92f260632a51e74461f6d117b4760207f273c56fa6dcab9e86d5a9ec7bc996178a2
U.S. factory, construction data brighten economic outlook

- Reuters

A new report shows that factory activity in the United States “increased more than expected in December . . . a further sign of strong economic momentum at the end of 2017.” Manufacturers are upbeat and anticipate a “ramp-up” in orders during the next year, the Institute for Supply Management report says.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d8442452e9fd66dd8989b05b63c73f532194c80939e52d9d9155eb073e5a97f6e0bc4a4aca809b888a818d133aa4d886970467f411287b4c

Trump’s Homeland Security chief pledges help with North Bay fire recovery

- San Francisco Chronicle

President Donald J. Trump’s new Secretary of Homeland Security Kirstjen Nielsen toured northern California’s wildfire devastation this week, vowing that “the White House would fully back recovery efforts.” The secretary’s “stop in Southern California coincided with the Trump administration’s disaster declaration for the area,” the Chronicle reports.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d8442452e9fd66dd6185af4588430c95319ec601d617b9a9016c6907c6d9a4c34e9b003e398d035c603abb1190d19cf31d21000193f94ad2
US construction spending hit record high in November

- The Associated Press

Private construction spending hit an all-time high in November of 2017, according to a new report from the Commerce Department. “Builders spent 0.8 percent more on construction projects in November, the fourth consecutive monthly gain,” The Associated Press reports.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d8442452e9fd66ddf0b4b4d5eae606132ff67956401bd29358513bfcb5ea26940dce14995ba92e50898bf30b9ba33c46b01a00185057f564

ICE is increasing presence in California because of 'sanctuary state' law, says Trump immigration chief

- The Los Angeles Times

Thomas Homan, acting director of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, said that the Trump Administration is preparing to “significantly increase” the agency’s enforcement presence in California. The move comes as a result of California’s new “sanctuary state” law, which took effect New Year’s Day. “If the politicians in California don’t want to protect their communities then ICE will,” Homan said.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d8442452e9fd66dda1b31e2a58c374108af1dd4870f78553c21c0f256772457b3ec66a8bae449f753559cb8558fe9203112136cc79be7f03
Food stamp rolls decline by more than 2 million in FY 2017

- The Washington Free Beacon

The number of people receiving food stamps fell by more than two million during the 2017 fiscal year, according to data recently released by the Agriculture Department. Secretary of Agriculture Sonny Perdue has implemented new policies intended to streamline access and efficiency within the program, the Free Beacon writes.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d8442452e9fd66dddfaf15aaf868d4e496aef1636144d89f8fe666fe6ba1527e47e09b716a199e083c5eaab4f20fc72697a32ab366f3617b
Poll: Voters show massive support for Trump immigration policies

- Breitbart

A recent NumbersUSA poll of likely 2018 voters showed overwhelming support for immigration policies that put America first. The poll is significant because it “asked respondents to pick from competing priorities, not just whether they want to welcome more immigrants,” Breitbart reports.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d8442452e9fd66dd49c6b606ba5ece89b77d239fa241cbf66ea812cdf71efc5924f30357a50746a38544953c96117681feb5128ccbc63cea
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d8442452e9fd66dd5653d0032b4f70f9b30cc77e45c911bb17c72f0cbc97765a911d86632a72a463a980602017798b39096109708c2f0c37
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4c6b46c9af5a32b07c55a329d4f8b66e89349f3d3ab1e5a2d7b37efee5f09c9b528211e0354fb4736594c20ee60f48bac
Unsubscribe

Trump’s Homeland Security chief pledges help with North Bay fire recovery - San Francisco Chronicle President Donald J. Trump’s new Secretary of Homeland Security Kirstjen Nielsen toured northern California’s wildfire devastation this week, vowing that “the White House would fully back recovery efforts.” The secretary’s “stop in Southern California coincided with the Trump administration’s disaster declaration for the area,” the Chronicle reports. US construction spending hit record high in November - The Associated Press Private construction spending hit an all-time high in November of 2017, according to a new report from the Commerce Department. “Builders spent 0.8 percent more on construction projects in November, the fourth consecutive monthly gain,” The Associated Press reports. ICE is increasing presence in California because of 'sanctuary state' law, says Trump immigration chief - The Los Angeles Times Thomas Homan, acting director of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, said that the Trump Administration is preparing to “significantly increase” the agency’s enforcement presence in California. The move comes as a result of California’s new “sanctuary state” law, which took effect New Year’s Day. “If the politicians in California don’t want to protect their communities then ICE will,” Homan said. Food stamp rolls decline by more than 2 million in FY 2017 - The Washington Free Beacon The number of people receiving food stamps fell by more than two million during the 2017 fiscal year, according to data recently released by the Agriculture Department. Secretary of Agriculture Sonny Perdue has implemented new policies intended to streamline access and efficiency within the program, the Free Beacon writes. Poll: Voters show massive support for Trump immigration policies - Breitbart A recent NumbersUSA poll of likely 2018 voters showed overwhelming support for immigration policies that put America first. The poll is significant because it “asked respondents to pick from competing priorities, not just whether they want to welcome more immigrants,” Breitbart reports.

name: Donald J. Trump
subject: RESPONSIBLE immigration reform
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J
body:
What are the terms of our proposal? |
---
| | | | | | | | [ ](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=fe3514384e20132081784af1474bb8c4fa72d7f9356a68d0de592a048db69f57688088f6768bca1efb1370b20cb75db92cfd1a9d5cfe1b0d)
---

Friend,

For years, politicians have talked about "compassionate" immigration reform, but every one of their proposals showed ZERO compassion for American workers, the victims of illegal immigration crime, and for the safety and security of our nation.

It's time for RESPONSIBLE immigration reform that actually puts the interests of American _citizens_ first.

Before I bring a proposal to the table, I want to have the full force of the American people united behind a plan that we want. I want to hear your immigration demands,
Friend.

**[Please take my Responsible Immigration Survey by MIDNIGHT TONIGHT.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=fe3514384e20132045e3f06038310b4239b273d1bb7e20ae77f4830b277e660a3a57c2afb31b946fec6af54a5e9d95f9a9010b6bb6e97ffb)**

Chuck and Nancy -- and EVERY liberal obstructionist -- need to understand that it's not just me that wants these critical proposals turned into law...

...These are the demands of the American PEOPLE. Your elected officials need to know just how many voters will be furious if our demands are not met.

_And I want to be able to give an exact number of how many people back each proposal. This is the Art of the Deal!_

**[So please, take my Responsible Immigration Survey by MIDNIGHT TONIGHT.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=fe3514384e201320c18302d67e5d84d734ddb97c8fe89664cc4eb1ca955fd9c526ac7c7d5f26dbccbcdaa7d7a3c173dcae3006bedcface17)**

Thank you,

[](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=fe3514384e201320e8a140ed62e5a43269dde1f44b5bf0209ff13e881fb2600e2056add3c5affa2bd5ddbd9018c7ad7572b3a39bd152b4ac)
Donald J. Trump
President of the United States

[TAKE THE SURVEY](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=fe3514384e201320b668ec11e874caffb68f595317afd41aa1e0602b3f8b005a98d9c0b9afdde54a12cc26bbaa74f7ae0202f9839f3018d4)
---

Contributions to the Trump Make America Great Again Committee are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J. Trump for President, Inc. and the Republican National Committee. You are receiving this email at dpwillis67@gmail.com
Trump Make America Great Again Committee, 138 Conant Street, 2nd Floor, Beverly, MA 01915

We believe this is an important way to reach our grassroots supporters with the most up-to-date information regarding the efforts of the Trump Make America Great Again Committee and President Trump, and we're glad you're on our team. It's because of grassroots supporters like you that we will Make America Great Again, and we appreciate your support. Thank you for all that you do!

[Privacy Policy](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=fe3514384e20132041f09f9b5c0f487aa0914e47dd8512336ab1a43a3cac6cb8bcdf0c787552724a4da4775b457e3b7ef8b8a539f99e0836) | [Unsubscribe ](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/unsub_center.aspx?qs=9b3e05efd8bd05fca0651da931c271d374a831665cc1c765aa8f0e456506efdde1ff9ac4e217f2c31c83aaa004fc1e1c82964aa7b828fd37) | [Update Profile](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/profile_center.aspx?qs=9b3e05efd8bd05fccf6ebf77cd5e8ea547e65639e0dc256c35e23b8cc0e47cb2c7b39b0fd82c11ddb3c0cd0eb1dc22177f5179dda31535fc)
---

Before I bring a proposal to the table, I want to have the full force of the American people united behind a plan that we want. I want to hear your immigration demands, Friend. name: Elect Senate Women (via DSCC.org)
subject: These two women are the 🔑 to winning the Senate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
The GOP majority just got one vote smaller -- and now, we have two Democratic women who could erase the Republican majority entirely.
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180106-ElectSW-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Team,

With Senator Doug Jones' swearing-in this week, the GOP majority just got one vote smaller -- and now, we have two Democratic women who could erase the Republican majority entirely.

**If we hold on to our current Senate seats and elect Nevada 's Jacky Rosen and Arizona's Kyrsten Sinema, we'll flip the Senate.**

But it will be no easy feat -- and Jacky and Kyrsten need each one of us working our hardest throughout 2018 to make it a reality.

[**Pitch in $1 right now to the Women 's Senate Network to re-elect HALF the women in the Senate this year and give Jacky and Kyrsten the resources they need to flip the Senate.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180106-ElectSW-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

We know winning back the Senate won't be easy -- but even Mitch McConnell says himself that "51-49 is a pretty tight majority."

And our best hope for winning back the majority lies in electing these two amazing Democratic women.

**So, let 's give Jacky, Kyrsten and all our Senate women the strongest possible start to 2018. Pitch in $1 now to kick off the election year and prepare to win back the Senate:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/elect-women-to-flip-the-senate**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180106-ElectSW-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Thank you,

Team DSCC

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d721/2b07114c/a4fe5458/731a1347/3830641876/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d721/2b07114c/a4fe5458/731a1346/3830641876/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d721/2b07114c/a4fe5458/731a1341/3830641876/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). We greatly appreciate your continued support. Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.
---

If we hold on to our current Senate seats and elect Nevada 's Jacky Rosen and Arizona's Kyrsten Sinema, we'll flip the Senate. But it will be no easy feat -- and Jacky and Kyrsten need each one of us working our hardest throughout 2018 to make it a reality. "= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=" We know winning back the Senate won't be easy -- but even Mitch McConnell says himself that "51-49 is a pretty tight majority." And our best hope for winning back the majority lies in electing these two amazing Democratic women. So, let 's give Jacky, Kyrsten and all our Senate women the strongest possible start to 2018. Pitch in $1 now to kick off the election year and prepare to win back the Senate: "= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=" Thank you, Team DSCC Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee 120 Maryland Ave. NE Washington, DC 20002 Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values. If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, "". If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please "". Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please "". We greatly appreciate your continued support. Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: Elect Ben Jealous for Maryland Governor
body:
Ben's campaign is facing its first fundraising deadline of 2018
#
Friends,
Ben Jealous' priority in 2018 is to flip the Maryland governor's mansion from Republican control back to the people, and he is going to do it by running a campaign committed to these fundamental values:
* Medicare for All
* Negotiated drug prices
* $15 minimum wage
* Ending student debt and implementing universal pre-K
* Setting a deadline to attain 100% clean, renewable energy
* Ending the school-to-prison pipeline and addressing the opioid crisis

[**Ben's campaign is facing its first fundraising deadline of 2018 --can you chip in $5 to help him before the deadline with the State Board of Elections?**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad93/bed43eb/380b3c89/75fe9941/2747943999/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-jealous?refcode=em180106-full&noskip=true")
This race is one of our best opportunities to elect a progressive champion to a governor's seat in 2018. Ben is a coalition builder who served as the youngest NAACP President in history. He has led campaigns for marriage equality, immigrant rights, voting rights, the environment, abolishing the death penalty, and getting big money out of politics in Maryland. Just this week he stood up for Maryland children when many were forced to go to schools without heat.
Ben understands that when one person suffers, we all suffer. From working as a student organizer for the NAACP Legal Defense Fund to reporting for the _Jackson Advocate_ , Mississippi's oldest historically black newspaper, to exposing corruption at a state prison and fighting to keep the doors open at black colleges, Ben's commitment to justice and opportunity for all has a long track record. It is the synthesis of the values Our Revolution looks for in candidates for office.
[**Please make a $5 contribution to Ben Jealous' campaign for Governor of Maryland before their first fundraising deadline of 2018. Together, we can win this seat back from Republican control and implement progressive policies to make life better for all Marylanders.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad93/bed43eb/380b3c89/75fe9941/2747943999/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-jealous?refcode=em180106-full&noskip=true")
Part of building a progressive America is electing progressive champions who will use the people's power to advance public policies that make a real difference--Ben Jealous is that champion.
He needs your support--[**donate today.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad93/bed43eb/380b3c89/75fe9941/2747943999/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-jealous?refcode=em180106-full&noskip=true")
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution
**[CONTRIBUTE»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad93/bed43eb/380b3c89/75fe9941/2747943999/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-jealous?refcode=em180106-full&noskip=true")**
PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
603 2ND ST, NE
WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a 501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution's general mission and are not designated for any specific activity.
603 2nd St NE, Washington, DC 20002

Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad93/bed43eb/380b3c89/75fe9943/2747943999/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjIyMTIzIn0=/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/OR-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad93/bed43eb/380b3c89/75fe9942/2747943999/VEsB/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/unsubscribe/"). Help us continue transforming American politics and fighting for progressive candidates and causes by [contributing to Our Revolution here](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad93/bed43eb/380b3c89/75fe994d/2747943999/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-footer-OR").

name: =?utf-8?Q?Team=20Scott?=
subject: ICYMI: Vermont Has The Potential, But Will We Seize Upon It? -- St. Albans Messenger
body:
Gov. Phil Scott’s state-of-the-state address had a core message, and one that sets it apart from that of prior governors...

View this email in your browser (http://mailchi.mp/philscott/icymi-vermont-has-the-potential-but-will-we-seize-upon-it-st-albans-messenger?e=19cebfe173)
https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=23f8f563d4&e=19cebfe173 Share (https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1d75f4d483&e=19cebfe173)
https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=db3f858891&e=19cebfe173 http%3A%2F%2Fmailchi.mp%2Fphilscott%2Ficymi-vermont-has-the-potential-but-will-we-seize-upon-it-st-albans-messenger Tweet (https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9b134e0d64&e=19cebfe173 http%3A%2F%2Fmailchi.mp%2Fphilscott%2Ficymi-vermont-has-the-potential-but-will-we-seize-upon-it-st-albans-messenger)
http://us13.forward-to-friend.com/forward?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8ab24a6722&e=19cebfe173 Forward to a Friend (http://us13.forward-to-friend.com/forward?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8ab24a6722&e=19cebfe173)
In Case You Missed It:

"Vermont Has the Potential, But Will We Seize Upon It?"

By Emerson Lynn
St. Albans Messenger
January 15, 2018

Gov. Phil Scott’s state-of-the-state address had a core message, and one that sets it apart from that of prior governors: We are managing a state of decline, which means to continue present practices does nothing to correct present trends. It’s a message almost no one wants to hear because reversing trends means disrupting what we are doing. It means taking away before we can add to.

Mr. Scott made it clear what the state faces: “We’re seeing workers age out of the labor force faster than we can replace them. Here are a few facts to consider:
* We have 23,000 fewer people under the age of 20 than we did in the year 2000.
* We now have nearly 30,000 more over the age of 65 than we did in that same year.
* There are 30,000 fewer people between the ages of 25 and 24 than we had 10 years ago.
* Ours is the second oldest population in the country. If we do nothing, we’ll soon be number one.
* And, think about this: Outside of Chittenden County, we are just three or four years from having just one worker for every retire, child or dependent of the state.”

This has got to stop. It’s simply unsustainable.”

As expected, critics pounced. They said it was a contradiction to say the state needs to do more and then refuse to spend more. When the governor said it was time “to stop digging” meaning the need to refuse to spend more, critics responded saying the refusal to spend more was a refusal to think ahead and to plan. They labeled his pledge to oppose higher taxes and fees as unrealistic.

At this point, the Democrats have not fielded a credible candidate to oppose Mr. Scott next November. There is a reason for that absence. Mr. Scott holds the high ground on the affordability argument. Vermonters have an intuitive sense that the resources are there, that we spend enough, that the answer involves a sustained effort to reallocate what we have in ways that produce better results.

Mr. Scott makes that point with education. We spend $1.6 billion each year to educate 76,000 students, which is 30,000 students fewer than we educated two decades ago. We average over $18,000 in spending per pupil, which is sixth highest in the nation.

New Hampshire ranks 11th, making it a state that also spends far above the national average. But if we spent what New Hampshire spends, we would spend roughly $304 million less each year. [New Hampshire also does as well, if not better, on student outcomes.] That $304 million dollars could be used in a variety of ways. It could be used to lower property taxes, making the state more affordable. It could be used for early childhood education, which, going forward would reduce social service costs and offer a better prepared workforce. It could be used to lower tuition for higher education, or for improvements in workforce training and technical education, all of which would improve our demographic outlook.

The same can be said of the state’s Agency of Human Services, which operates a $2.5 billion budget. When we work to improve the health, etc., of the disadvantaged, or the poor then we’re also working to reduce what we spend in future years. Think about the potential. If we are spending $2.5 billion each year [this includes federal funding] that means we are spending almost exactly $4,000 for each of the 625,000 people who live here. We spend 50 percent more than the national average for human services and we rank third highest nationally.

Vermont is also spends more on a per capita basis than any other state when it comes to health care spending. The tally is over $5 billion annually [including money from the feds] for that same 625,000 people.

Education, human services and health care have a number of common denominator factors that can be forged together to attack the central goal of making us a healthier, happier, safer and more attractive place to live and work.

But it will never work to treat each separately. It will never work to simply add more resources to each and keep the objectives in place. That works for the advocates, but not for the state as a whole. We’re at that spot where we can no longer deny the inevitability of what lies before us if we don’t change. That was the governor’s message. There will be those who prefer denial, and those who are not comfortable saying no. That’s unfortunate. We do have the resources to do whatever we choose. It just takes leadership and the belief that good policy ends up being good politics.
https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=57e832d99a&e=19cebfe173
https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=18cfb4ce99&e=19cebfe173
https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=70e5023d68&e=19cebfe173

============================================================
Phil Scott for Vermont, Glen Wright, Treasurer,
P.O. Box 988, Montpelier, VT, 05602

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can ** update your preferences (https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/profile?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=75128fb49c&e=19cebfe173)
or ** unsubscribe from this list (https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=75128fb49c&e=19cebfe173&c=8ab24a6722)
